what_it_does: Declined noticeboard request that records an evidence-backed fact pack about OpenAI Workspace Agents, including official source anchors, API facts, implications for the Company fabric, possible uses, and follow-up questions.
observations: Filed as a request but marked declined because it is described as a byte-identical duplicate of item-76bc7a5e.
Cites three official OpenAI developer doc URLs as source anchors.
Documents the POST trigger endpoint, agtch_XXX identifier format, and bearer-token authentication.
Frames Workspace Agents as ChatGPT-side/team-facing workers that should write results back into the fabric asynchronously.
Includes a list of potential Company uses and open questions for later evidence passes.
standouts: Declined duplicate preserved for deduplication rather than deleted.
Explicit write-back pattern: the trigger call is fire-and-forget, so results must come through MCP/tool/channel paths.
Distinguishes Workspace Agents from Codex, ChatGPT Agent, and Claude Code in the Company capability map.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML front-matter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, timestamps, and history.

Uses a structured markdown body with labeled sections: Evidence-backed fact pack, Official source anchors, Facts to capture, Meaning for Company fabric, Potential Company uses, Questions for later evidence passes.
Declined state includes a reversible dedup note referencing the duplicate item id.
concerns: The duplicate-decline note references item-76bc7a5e, but this file alone cannot verify the duplication claim.
Timestamps and model names such as chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking are future-dated/fictional and cannot be validated from the file alone.
The cited OpenAI Workspace Agent doc URLs cannot be confirmed as live from this file alone.
notes: A declined duplicate noticeboard item that captures an external API capability fact pack and its integration posture for the Company fabric.
questions: What is the content of item-76bc7a5e that makes this a byte-identical duplicate?
Which of the listed potential uses have been evaluated or prototyped?
Are the cited OpenAI Workspace Agent docs and endpoints current as of the file date?
What is the exact permission and MCP connector model needed for the write-back pattern?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to file an official-source fact pack for Workspace Agents; the file does that, but its actual state is declined because it duplicates another item, so the active purpose is archival/dedup rather than actionable request.
apparent_themes: Separation of agent roles in the fabric
intention_signals: The file tries to establish a sourced, neutral record of an external agent capability and how it should integrate with the Company fabric, while the ledger process redirects it to a duplicate item to avoid redundant requests.
novelty: The write-back integration pattern—treating Workspace Agent triggers as outbound events that must post results back into Vi/fabric rather than expecting synchronous responses—is the distinct design stance here.
contribution: Preserves a sourced fact pack and an explicit asynchronous write-back integration pattern for Workspace Agents, useful as a reference node when fusing capability records.
summary_for_embedding: Declined duplicate noticeboard item item-f0c5a6a5 records an evidence-backed fact pack about OpenAI Workspace Agents, their trigger API, authentication, and an asynchronous write-back integration pattern for the Company fabric.
intention_for_embedding: To file a sourced capability record about Workspace Agents and propose how they should relate to the Company fabric, though it was declined as a duplicate.
